description: Note (the sender calls it a 'khidma') in Arabic script. Instructing the addressee to give 'the thing which I told you about' to the bearer of the note. Then, "as soon as you receive this khidma, send after the 'aṣḥāb al-dawāwīn' and they should come to you." The continuation is not clear, and it may be interspersed with unrelated jottings (several names and Greek/Coptic numerals). It seems as draft of a letter to a higher authority, and the 'khidma' could refer to a petition. Reused on recto for Hebrew poetry.
description_he: פתק (הכותב קורא לו 'ח'דמה') בכתב ערבי; מורה לנמען לתת את 'הדבר שסיפרתי לך' לנושא הפתק; שמש מחדש צד א' לשירה עברית
